Content: THE Signal Acquisition module SHALL read the VehSpd signal from the CAN input buffer and perform range validation against [0 kph to 250 kph] within [40 ms] of reception, flagging F_OOR_HI_VEHSPD when the value exceeds the upper bound and F_OOR_LO_VEHSPD when the value is below the lower bound.

THE Timeout Monitor module SHALL start a watchdog timer on each valid reception of VehSpd and SHALL set F_TO_VEHSPD if no new sample of VehSpd arrives within [50 ms], notifying the Fault Manager within [5 ms] of expiry.

THE Alive Counter Monitor module SHALL compare the VehSpd AliveCounter value of the current cycle with the previous cycle and SHALL set F_ALC_VEHSPD if the value has not incremented for [3 consecutive cycles], reporting the fault to the Fault Manager within [5 ms].

THE CRC Validation module SHALL compute the CRC-8 checksum over the received VehSpd message payload and SHALL set F_CRC_VEHSPD within [5 ms] if the computed value does not match the transmitted CRC byte.

WHEN the Fault Manager receives any active fault from {F_OOR_HI_VEHSPD, F_OOR_LO_VEHSPD, F_TO_VEHSPD, F_ALC_VEHSPD, F_CRC_VEHSPD}, THE Reaction module SHALL command Torque = 0 Nm, disable LKA, activate the driver Warning, and set the corresponding DTC within [10 ms] of fault confirmation.
Safe State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON

Content: WHILE F_TO_VEHSPD is active, THE Recovery module SHALL monitor VehSpd validity and SHALL clear F_TO_VEHSPD plus extinguish DTC_101_VSPD_TO within [15 ms] once VehSpd has been continuously valid for [500 ms].

Content: THE Diagnostic Trigger module SHALL evaluate the TurnSignal enum range per SYS_REQ_0029 and shall forward out-of-range values to the Fault Manager for further classification.
THE Diagnostic Event Manager module SHALL log all confirmed and healed faults with a timestamp and cycle count into non-volatile memory for post-drive diagnostic readout.
THE Software Initialization module SHALL configure all signal input buffers, timers, and fault flags to their default power-on state within [100 ms] of ECU reset.

THE Steering Actuator Interface module SHALL reject any commanded torque request outside the actuator's mechanical limit of plus or minus 15 Nm and SHALL saturate the output to the nearest valid limit.
